title: Baseten closes $1.5B Series F at $13B valuation as inference-infra spending compounds
subtitle: The AI inference startup's fourth raise in 18 months lands with Altimeter, Conviction, and Spark leading — 20x YoY revenue growth and over a billion daily inference requests underlying the round.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Baseten announced a $1.5 billion Series F this week, structured across two tranches at $13 billion and $11 billion valuations. Altimeter Capital, Conviction, and Spark Capital led, with Sands Capital and Wellington Management joining as co-leads. IVP, Greylock, Battery, D.E. Shaw Ventures, and existing investors also participated. The company reported roughly 20x year-over-year revenue growth and said its platform now serves over one billion inference requests per day across 87 clusters spanning 18 cloud environments. This is Baseten's fourth raise in eighteen months and brings total funding above two billion dollars.

Baseten's product is unglamorous and load-bearing: the runtime layer that gets a trained model into a production API endpoint reliably and cheaply. That is the layer where actual AI deployments live or die, and it is the layer that has been most under-invested relative to model training. A $13 billion valuation for an inference platform is a market statement that serving is now the constraint, not training. Compute-per-inference has fallen roughly 10x in two years, but query volume has grown far faster, so the aggregate spend keeps expanding.

The round fits into a two-week pattern of infrastructure-first megadeals. Together AI closed $800 million in early July with Aramco participating. Baseten closes $1.5 billion. Both companies operate in the same layer of the stack: routing, scaling, and cost-optimizing model serving across heterogeneous compute. The venture market is voting that inference is where the durable margins are — because model weights are increasingly commoditized, but the operational surface between weights and end users is not.

A takeaway for learners: if you want to build a career in AI infrastructure, the roles are not all at the frontier labs. The companies quietly running production inference for those labs' customers — Baseten, Together, Fireworks, Replicate, Modal — are the ones with the biggest hiring gaps and the least crowded talent markets. Understanding GPU scheduling, KV-cache management, quantization tradeoffs, and inference-server internals is now as valuable as knowing how to fine-tune a model.
